figures.caption: Fig. 4: OmniCAM dataset construction. Left: UE5 synthetic videos provide accurate camera poses to build paired and triplet supervision. Right: real videos are processed via trajectory estimation, trajectory filtering, trajectory classification, and intra-class matching to obtain reliable trajectories and cross-scene motion pairs.
Fig. 6: Qualitative results for text-controlled camera motion.

Fig. 11: Qualitative ablation. (a) Removing DC leads to failed camera motions and degraded visual quality. (b)(c) Omitting CC results in structural distortions in I2V and content hallucinations in V2V (red boxes). (d) Training solely on synthetic data ensures precise control but lacks photorealism.

Fig. 2: Compositional camera control with multi-modal conditions. OmniCamera enables flexible combinations of multiple conditioning inputs, such as overlaying text-guided camera motion on top of trajectory or reference-video control, efficiently extending and diversifying camera motion effects.
Table 8: Predefined camera-motion library: 20 basic and 30 composite types.

Fig. 9: Visual comparison of OmniCamera with state-of-the-art methods. Text Control: Wan2.2-Fun-Camera produces some artifacts (e.g., the “ear” mutating into “eye”). Trajectory Control: ReCamMaster [2] produces severe distortions on the “bus” and “motorcyclist”. Reference-Video Control: CamCloneMaster [26] executes incorrect camera motion while introducing heavy background and object distortions.

Fig. 5: Dual-level curriculum data composition. Condition-level curriculum progresses from text control (Stage I) to reference-video control (Stage II) to trajectory control (Stage III), following a coarse-to-fine and easy-to-hard order. In Stage II and Stage III, data-level curriculum first trains on synthetic data with precise trajectories (Substage 1), then fine-tunes on real-world videos to recover photorealism (Substage 2).

Fig. 12: Visualization of the effect of Condition RoPE. Without Condition RoPE, the model exhibits strong interference between positional encoding and injected conditions, leading to unstable and degraded outputs. Applying Condition RoPE yields significantly more coherent and robust generation.

Fig. 3: Pipeline of OmniCamera. Left: Diffusion Transformer with decoupled condition injection: text and reference video tokens are concatenated for self-attention; trajectory features are added to the noise latent. Right: Condition RoPE explicitly disentangles modalities using unique positional encodings.
